title: Ctenitis inaequalis
intro: Ctenitis inaequalis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Ctenitis inaequalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Ctenitis inaequalis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Ctenitis inaequalis belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is a species of fern that is native to certain regions. It is classified within the genus Ctenitis, which includes various species of ferns. Ctenitis inaequalis is further classified into the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The specific classification of Ctenitis inaequalis helps in understanding its characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Ctenitis inaequalis has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and infections. In some parts of the world, it is used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, its leaves are used in traditional medicine to lower fever and alleviate headaches. However, more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.

These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been reported to have potential anti-cancer and cardiovascular protective effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological potential of these active constituents.
